_provenance.description: Representative InclusionProof.toJSON() fixture exercising long step paths (originally surfaced as the >256-bit SMT path failure mode reported in issue #295). After issue #295 rewrite #2, UXF treats the SmtPath as an opaque STS-canonical CBOR blob and imposes no bit-length constraint — this fixture now verifies that UXF forwards the bytes verbatim regardless of step-path bit-width. Other fields (root, authenticator, unicityCertificate) are synthetic but well-formed. See tests/unit/uxf/hash.real-inclusion-proof.test.ts.

step_path_bit_lengths.1: 256 bits (2^256 - 1) — value at the historical UXF-imposed limit (no longer enforced — STS owns the bound)
step_path_bit_lengths.2: 259 bits — exact decimal extracted from the user-reported sphere.telco migration failure
step_path_bit_lengths.4: 280 bits — beyond STS's current bit-length range; verifies UXF imposes no upper bound (STS may, or may not, accept it independently)
